Fast Cars, Danger, Fire and Knives.notes: EP originally packaged with The Living Human Curiosity Sideshow lyric book.

Cat Food.notes: Two vocal tracks plus instrumentals; Cat Food first issued as a limited 7-inch with the Kid Robot toy collab.

Freedom Finger (Music from the Game).notes: Music written for the Freedom Finger video game: 3 vocal tracks plus instrumentals.

The Recycling Bin.notes: Aesop Rock x Blockhead EP: Blockhead remixes of Pigs, Defender and Kodokushi (plus instrumentals), a Garbology companion release.

Tugboat Complex.notes: Compilation appearance: The Tugboat Complex on Inside Out Vol. 1 - A Full Blown Compilation (2000); self-produced per the Aesop Rock discography production-credits table. Part of the Tugboat Complex series (Pt. 2 on Appleseed, Pt. 3 on Labor Days).

The Active Element.notes: Early one-off track circa 2000 that circulates on compilations/bootlegs (e.g. Unreleased, Compilations & Features); no authoritative personnel source found.

Kill 'em All.notes: Compilation appearance: Kill 'em All on Definitive Jux Presents (2001); self-produced per the Aesop Rock discography production-credits table.

Wise Up.notes: Compilation appearance: Wise Up on Tags of the Times 3 (Mary Joy Recordings, 2001); self-produced per the Aesop Rock discography production-credits table.

Dead Pan.notes: Single-track contribution to the compilation Definitive Jux Presents 2 (Definitive Jux, 2002); self-produced per the production-credits table in the Aesop Rock discography Wikipedia article.

Train Buffer.notes: From Chocolate Industries' Urban Renewal Program comp; also the B-side of the El-P/Aesop Rock split 12" Dead Light / Train Buffer (CHLT 028). Discogs scopes Mr. Len's producer credit to the El-P side (A1-A3) and carries an unscoped El-P producer credit read as covering Train Buffer; not in Aesop's self-production credits table.

Inner City Hustle.notes: Track 3 on Embedded Studios Presents: The Bedford Files (Embedded Music, 2002). Discogs release-level credits give beats/production on track 3 to Hipsta (Dave Schur); lyrics by Ian Bavitz and Victor Roscoe (L.I.F.E. Long).

Numb (To the Guns).notes: Track 12 on The Prof. - Convexed (Nature Sounds, 2003); Discogs release producer credit for The Prof. covers it. Also issued on the Nature Sounds Presents The Prof. In... Convexed Sampler 12".

Dragon Coaster.notes: Sources conflict: the Aesop Rock discography Wikipedia article places it on Atlantis Sessions & The Deadly Art of All Day Brainstorming w/ Mojo, but the Discogs entry for that comp only carries Shelshock's 'Dragon Coaster 2' (feat. Blueprint/Vast Aire, no Aesop). Werner Von Wallenrod's fan discography files the original as Dujeous/Shelshocked Productions (Wax Poetic, 2003) w/ Mojo & P-Love. Producer credit not fully confirmed.

Miss By a Mile.notes: Track 9 on Mike Nardone Presents We Came From Beyond Volume 2 (Razor & Tie, 2003); Discogs lists the title as 'Miss By A Smile' with an explicit Blockhead producer credit.

All in All.notes: Single-track contribution to Definitive Jux Presents 3 (2004); self-produced per the production-credits table in the Aesop Rock discography Wikipedia article.

Preservation.notes: Track 16 on Dreddy Krueger Presents... Think Differently Music: Wu-Tang Meets the Indie Culture (Babygrande, 2005); the track shares its name with its producer, Preservation.

Junkyard.notes: Contribution to the NBA 2K6 video-game soundtrack ('NBA 2K6: The Tracks' / @2K6); self-produced per the production-credits table in the Aesop Rock discography Wikipedia article.

Lice.notes: Lice = Aesop Rock + Homeboy Sandman. 5-track EP self-released as a free download, November 30, 2015; one outside producer per track.

Lice Two: Still Buggin'.notes: 5-track free EP (September 29, 2016). Bandcamp credits production across the EP to Dan Hayden, Mondee, Aesop Rock, SoberMindedMusiC and Mono En Stereo without an itemized per-track split.

Triple Fat Lice.notes: Third Lice EP (a.k.a. Lice 3: Triple Fat Lice), released August 11, 2017; one producer per track; Ben Boogz is of 2 Hungry Bros.

Miami Lice Season Four.notes: Fourth Lice EP (Miami Lice: Season Four), 8 tracks, released March 11, 2026; produced entirely by Aesop Rock, written/performed by both, mixed by Joey Raia.

Are You Gonna Eat That?.notes: Hail Mary Mallon = Aesop Rock + Rob Sonic + DJ Big Wiz. Debut album (Rhymesayers, 2011); all writing credited to Hail Mary Mallon; personnel adapted from liner notes per Wikipedia.

Bestiary.notes: Second Hail Mary Mallon album (Rhymesayers, 2014). Deluxe edition adds remixes by Edison ('Used Cars') and Blockhead ('The Red List').

Hokey Fright.notes: The Uncluded = Aesop Rock + Kimya Dawson (Rhymesayers, 2013). Every part of every song performed by the duo except James McNew's (Yo La Tengo) drums on 'Delicate Cycle'. Deluxe edition adds a Blockhead remix of 'That Cat Has Worms'.

Malibu Ken.notes: Malibu Ken = Aesop Rock + TOBACCO (Black Moth Super Rainbow). Debut album (Rhymesayers, 2019); all lyrics written by Aesop Rock (Ian Bavitz), all production by Tobacco.
